21.reason: Error: Using %d prints the integer value of j (e.g., 49 for '1') instead of the character. Should use %c to print the character.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Using %d prints the integer value of j (e.g., 49 for '1') instead of the character. Should use %c to print the character.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Using %d prints the integer value of j (e.g., 48 for '0'), not the character. Should use %c to print the digit character.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Using %d prints the ASCII value of the digit character, not the digit character itself. Should be %c.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: using %d prints ASCII value, should be %c to print character
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The format specifier "%d" prints the integer ASCII value of the character instead of the character itself. It should be "%c" to correctly output the expanded digits.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The format specifier "%d" prints the ASCII integer value of the character. It should be "%c" to print the actual digit character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Using %d prints the integer value (ASCII code) instead of the character; should use %c to output digit characters]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Using %d prints the integer value (ASCII code) instead of the character; should use %c to output digit characters]
